The '''Emperor's cane''' was a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifact]] in the form of a glossy black cane, best known for its use by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the later years of his reign, in the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==History==
[[File:PalpatinesCane.jpg|thumb|left|A [[Unidentified Outer Rim cyberneticist|cyberneticist]] with the Sith artifact.]]

Many centuries before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="CSWEN">{{CSWECite|II|394|Nubyl}}</ref> the cane had been stolen by a [[Unidentified Outer Rim cyberneticist|cyberneticist]] who worked out of an industrial [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] wasteland [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. This scientist, realizing that a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Unidentified Mandalorian clan (Ung Kusp)|clan]] loyal to [[Ung Kusp]] had been hired to recover the cane, offered to outfit [[Jaing]] and [[Durge/Legends|Durge]], who were enemies of Kusp's clan, with armor incorporating advanced weaponry and reflex systems if they protected him. Shortly after the operations were complete, a Mandalorian strike team broke into the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist's]] building and attacked Jaing, Durge, and the scientist's assistant [[Nubyl]] in the form of a defense cyborg. The trio beat back the attackers until one of them threw a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]]. Jaing was [[Death/Legends|fatally]] wounded, Durge was knocked unconscious, and Nubyl malfunctioned.<ref name="Prototypes" />

The Mandalorians recovered the cane, but before they could leave, Durge awoke and, furious about Jaing's mortal wounding, defeated the Mandalorian raiders. The scientist took back the cane and advised Durge to exterminate the other Mandalorians in retribution, promising that he would revive Jaing. Durge set off to hunt Kusp's clan.<ref name="Prototypes" />

By [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],<ref name="1 BBY">"First Impressions" establishes that Palpatine had adopted the use of the cane by the time of [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa's]] ascension to the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]]. According to ''[[Children of the Jedi]]'', this occurred when Organa was eighteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of age, and ''The New Essential Chronology'' dates her birth to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. Therefore, "First Impressions" and Palpatine's use of the cane occur in 1 BBY.</ref> the cane had fallen into the hands of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="First Impressions">{{Tales|15|First Impressions (comic story)}}</ref> Palpatine often used the cane in order to appear to be weak instead of actually needing it, although the Dark Side corruption as well as old age had taken such a toll on his body that his movement actually was impaired when not actively drawing on the Force.<ref name="SW:TCVD">''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]''</ref><ref name="Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith novelization">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref> When he inspected the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] in the days preceding the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Palpatine carried the cane with him, and brought it into his [[Emperor's throne room|throne room]].<ref name="RotJ" />

Palpatine, six years after his resurrection on [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], utilized a similar cane when encountering a recently captured [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Skywalker]] on Byss.<ref name="Dark Empire 2">[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Emperor's cane first appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="RotJ" /> According to the reference book ''[[From Star Wars to Indiana Jones: The Best of the Lucasfilm Archives]]'', the original prop measured 20×94×3 centimeters.<ref name="From Star Wars to Indiana Jones: The Best of the Lucasfilm Archives">''[[From Star Wars to Indiana Jones: The Best of the Lucasfilm Archives]]''</ref>
